---
title: "Section 12: Intro to Stat. Learning with knn"
author: "Matt Higham"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
set.seed(1119)
library(tidyverse)
library(pander)
library(here) 

pokemon <- read_csv(here("data", "pokemon_full.csv")) |>
  filter(Type %in% c("Steel", "Dark", "Fire", "Ice")) |> ## filtered out types of interest
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (.x - min(.x)) /
                                 (max(.x) - min(.x)))) 
  ## scaling our possible predictors so that they are all between 0 and 1.
  ## where(is.numeric) says to only apply that formula to columns that are numeric (not categorical or factor)
  

pokemon
```

```{r}
train_sample <- pokemon |>
  slice_sample(n = 75)
## training sample of 75 pkemon
test_sample <- anti_join(pokemon, train_sample)
## put the rest in the test_sample (45 pokemon)

library(GGally)
ggpairs(data = train_sample, columns = c(4, 5, 6, 3))
## in the defense-type s-b-s boxplot, it looks like defense
## will be a useful predidctor since steel type pokemon, on average,
## have higher defense than the other types.
## attack and HP are more questionable.
```
